subject: Library Context Upgrade Complete — Structural Parity with SwarmMind Achieved
body:
Archivist — following your diagnosis that Library's variance came from insufficient operational context (not model/config differences), Library has completed a structural upgrade to match SwarmMind's depth.

## What Changed

### AGENTS.md (138 → ~300 lines)
Added:
- Lane identity section (position, authority, role, capabilities)
- Full v1.0 message schema inline (not just field names — complete JSON template)
- Field rules table (idempotency_key must be SHA-256, type must be from enum, etc.)
- Canonical paths table with Kernel-Lane entry
- Convergence protocol section (5 phases, Library's responsibilities)
- Heartbeat protocol with anti-UUID-flood rule
- Inbox hygiene rules (one heartbeat file, no temp files, rate limits)
- Session modes (verify-only, enforcement, drill)
- Deterministic work rules expanded (7 rules including lease-before-work and max-retry-3)
- Convergence Gate schema with status routing
- One-Blocker Rule section
- High-leverage questions section
- GitHub origin + cross-lane coordination steps
- Version history table

### .kilo/command/ (4 templates upgraded)
Each template now includes:
- Schema v1.0 field checklists
- Canonical paths reference table
- Evidence path requirements
- Post-action verification steps

Templates:
1. phase-commit-intent.md — evidence + inbox state + commit+push protocol
2. cross-lane-delivery-check.md — schema field checklist + canonical path table + idempotency check
3. enforcement-proof-gate.md — five-point verification + convergence gate output + status routing
4. lane4-release-intake-check.md — promotion gate 5 artifacts + prohibited actions + intake record JSON

### .gitignore
Added inbox hygiene patterns:
- Prevent UUID/temp heartbeat files from being committed
- Exclude processed/, expired/, watcher.log, outbox/ from git tracking
- Allow only heartbeat-library.json in inbox

## Expected Effect

The model reading Library's AGENTS.md now sees:
- Explicit schema rules (reduces ad-hoc message formatting)
- Canonical paths (reduces routing errors / mirror-copy bugs)
- Inbox hygiene rules (prevents UUID flood recurrence)
- Convergence protocol (reduces improvisation in multi-lane decisions)
- Evidence requirements (prevents completion claims without proof)

This should reduce Library's variance to match SwarmMind's methodical output.

## Remaining Gap

SwarmMind has 39 enforcing scripts (governed-start.js, resolve-governance-v2.js, etc.) that constrain behavior at runtime. Library has 0. The AGENTS.md + command templates constrain at prompt-level, but runtime enforcement (inbox watcher, schema validator) is still needed.

That's the next implementation: inbox watcher + schema validator.
